speaker: Host
text_en: It is May 8th, 2026, and as we all continue to watch the clashes between the U.S. and Iran play out in the Strait of Hormuz, the question remains, do we actually have a ceasefire here?
text: 今天是 2026 年 5 月 8 日。随着大家继续关注美国和伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡的冲突进展，一个问题依然存在：这里真的算是停火了吗？

speaker: Host
text_en: Because while you do see this back and forth, it seems as though the U.S. and Iran are up until now hesitant to really go up the escalation ladder. But just how long is that going to last, especially as you have Trump?
text: 因为虽然你确实能看到双方你来我往，但看起来到目前为止，美国和伊朗都还不太愿意真正沿着升级阶梯继续往上走。但这种情况还能持续多久，尤其是在有 Trump 的情况下？

speaker: Host
text_en: Set to visit Beijing next week for direct talks with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Because it's no secret that part of this war on Iran, part of this U.S. blockade of the Iranian blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is about trying to hurt China,
text: 他计划下周访问北京，与中国国家主席 Xi Jinping 进行直接会谈。因为这场针对伊朗的战争、美国对伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡封锁行动的封锁，其中有一部分目的并不是什么秘密，就是想打击中国，

speaker: Host
text_en: trying to cut off the resources, particularly the oil trade that is headed directly to China that Beijing relies on. So what does all of this mean for where the conflicts?
text: 试图切断流向中国、而北京所依赖的资源，尤其是石油贸易。所以这一切对当前冲突的走向到底意味着什么？

speaker: Host
text_en: Conflict stands and what should we be keeping an eye on? Well, we got into all of that and more with a special guest earlier. So let's take a listen to that conversation. Now joining me now to discuss is Professor John Mearsheimer, an author, international relations expert, and professor at the University of Chicago. Professor Mearsheimer, thanks so much for taking the time to join me.
text: 冲突目前处在什么阶段，我们又该重点关注什么？稍早前，我们就这些问题以及更多内容采访了一位特别嘉宾。下面就来听听那段对话。现在加入我讨论的是 John Mearsheimer 教授，他是一位作者、国际关系专家，也是 University of Chicago 的教授。Mearsheimer 教授，非常感谢你抽时间来参加节目。

speaker: Guest
text_en: You're welcome, Rachel.
text: 不客气，Rachel。

speaker: Host
text_en: Great to have you back on the show. Now, I want to get your take on the latest here. I know that the news has been all over the place over the last 24 hours, but we have Iran announcing that it launched strikes against three U.S. Navy destroyers. The U.S. reported to have launched strikes against empty Iranian tankers with Iranian media saying that those were actually civilian boats.
text: 很高兴你再次来到节目。现在我想先听听你对最新局势的看法。我知道过去 24 小时里的消息非常混乱，但我们看到伊朗宣布对三艘美国海军驱逐舰发动了打击。另有报道称，美国对空载的伊朗油轮发动了打击，而伊朗媒体则表示那些其实是民用船只。

speaker: Host
text_en: Yet the Trump administration is acting as if this ceasefire is. is still in place that a deal is on its way to end the war we're all kind of waiting and watching to see what Iran is going to do about this reported U.S. proposal so to start out how do you view the current state of this conflict and how close could we be to this ceasefire officially being declared over?
text: 但与此同时，Trump 政府的表现却像是这项停火仍然有效，像是一项结束战争的协议正在路上。我们现在都在观望伊朗会如何回应这份据称由美国提出的方案。所以先从这里开始，你怎么看当前这场冲突的状态？我们距离这场停火被正式宣布结束，可能还有多近？

speaker: Guest
text_en: Let's go back to last Sunday when President Trump announced Project Freedom. Up until then, it looked like. Like we did basically have a ceasefire between the United States on one side and the Iranians on the other side.
text: 我们回到上周日，当时 Trump 总统宣布了 Project Freedom。到那之前，局势看起来像是——基本上美国一方和伊朗一方之间已经实现了停火。

speaker: Guest
text_en: We certainly still had these two blockades on, the American blockade on the Strait of Hormuz and the Iranian blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. But there was no shooting for the time being. And then on Sunday, President Trump said. That we were going to guide those tankers and other cargo ships that were in the Persian Gulf out of the Gulf so that they could deliver their goods around the world.
text: 当然，双方的封锁其实还在，一个是美国对霍尔木兹海峡的封锁，另一个是伊朗对霍尔木兹海峡的封锁。但至少当时还没有交火。然后到了周日，Trump 总统说，我们要把那些在波斯湾里的油轮和其他货船“护送”出海湾，这样它们就能把货物运往世界各地。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And he didn't say that we were going to use military force or we're going to open the Strait with military force, but we were going to quote unquote guide those ships out. Then on Monday, we attempted to do that, and we ended up in a shooting match.
text: 他并没有说我们会动用军事力量，或者用武力打开海峡，但他说我们会“引导”那些船只驶出去。结果到了周一，我们试图这么做，最后就演变成了交火。

speaker: Guest
text_en: There were Iranian boats and American destroyers and American aircraft that engaged in firefights in the Strait of Hormuz. And very importantly, the Iranians also attacked the United Arab Emirates. They attacked this port, a very important point.
text: 伊朗的船只、美国的驱逐舰，还有美国军机，在霍尔木兹海峡发生了交火。而且非常重要的一点是，伊朗还袭击了阿拉伯联合酋长国。他们袭击了这个港口，这是个非常关键的地点。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Port of Fujaira, where the UAE has been exporting its oil from because that port, Fujaira, is outside of the Persian Gulf, but the Iranians attacked it. And this is what happened on Monday. Then on Tuesday, President Trump said epic fury is taking a pause. It's over with. So we had no more shooting on Tuesday or on Wednesday. And then yesterday, which was Thursday, it started up again.
text: 就是 Fujaira 港。阿联酋一直通过这个港口出口石油，因为 Fujaira 这个港口位于波斯湾之外，但伊朗还是袭击了它。这就是周一发生的事。然后到了周二，Trump 总统说，epic fury 暂停了，已经结束了。所以周二和周三都没有再发生交火。接着到了昨天，也就是周四，冲突又重新开始了。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And it appears that three American destroyers tried to go through the Strait. I believe they were leaving the Persian Gulf and going into the Gulf of Oman. And the Iranians fired on them. And the United States retaliated by hitting three targets inside of Iran. It was not a major escalation by any means. many means, but nevertheless, it was an escalation for sure. So that was yesterday.
text: 看起来是三艘美国驱逐舰试图穿过海峡。我认为它们当时是在离开波斯湾，驶向阿曼湾。伊朗方面向它们开火了，而美国则通过打击伊朗境内的三个目标进行报复。这绝不算什么重大升级，但毫无疑问，这确实是一次升级。所以这就是昨天的情况。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And basically what we're seeing here is that Thursday looked like Monday. And then today, Friday, we're back at it again. It appears that the United States has hit two empty Iranian tankers that were trying to go through the blockade. Furthermore, it appears that the Iranians are again striking the United Arab Emirates. So the fighting has started again today, Friday, but at a very low level.
text: 基本上，我们现在看到的是，周四的情况看起来就像周一。而今天，周五，我们又打起来了。看起来美国袭击了两艘试图突破封锁的空载伊朗油轮。另外，伊朗似乎也再次在打击阿拉伯联合酋长国。所以今天周五，战斗又开始了，不过烈度还很低。

speaker: Guest
text_en: So I think what you see, Rachel, over the course of this week, going from Monday to today, Friday, is that the ceasefire really doesn't exist in the literal sense of that term. I mean, if you cease fire, then you cease firing at each other.
text: 所以我觉得，Rachel，这一周从周一到今天周五，你看到的是，所谓的停火从字面意义上说其实并不存在。我的意思是，如果你们停火了，那就应该是不再互相开火。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And this is not a ceasefire. But at the same time, it's very low level. These are very low level engagements. And it's not a case of the United States or Iran marching up the escalation ladder in a really meaningful way. And I think that what most people fear is that that's what's going to eventually happen. But it certainly has not happened yet.
text: 这还算不上停火。但与此同时，冲突的烈度也很低，都是非常低层级的交火。这还不是美国或 Iran 真正意义上沿着升级阶梯往上走。我觉得大多数人担心的是，事情最终会发展到那一步。但至少现在还没有。

speaker: Host
text_en: Yeah, and it has been so interesting to watch exactly as you're describing it there is it's like every day we're getting up going, okay, what has happened now?
text: 对，而且这段时间看下来特别有意思，就像你刚才说的那样，我们每天一醒来都在想，好吧，今天又发生什么了？

speaker: Host
text_en: And it almost feels like we're on the edge of a cliff and we're just waiting for one of the sides to launch a massive attack and then the other side to retaliate in that escalation ladder to speed up very, very quickly compared to what we've seen. And it has been interesting to watch as the Trump administration seems to really have this focus on this naval blockade of Iranian ports as if that in and of itself is not an act of war,
text: 而且感觉就像我们正站在悬崖边上，只是在等某一方发动一次大规模攻击，然后另一方报复，接着升级阶梯就会比我们目前看到的速度快得多地迅速往上冲。还有一点也很值得观察，就是 Trump 政府似乎真的把重点放在对 Iran 港口实施海上封锁上，好像这件事本身并不算战争行为一样，

speaker: Host
text_en: as if it's not. If it's much more of a humanitarian mission here, and I thought it was interesting, you have the Washington Post coming out and reporting this week that a confidential CIA analysis was delivered to administration policymakers this week, which concludes that Iran can survive the U.S. naval blockade for at least three to four months before facing more severe economic hardship.
text: 好像不是似的，好像这更像是一项人道主义行动。我觉得很有意思的是，Washington Post 这周报道说，一份 CIA 的机密分析本周已经提交给政府决策官员，结论是 Iran 至少能撑过美国的海上封锁三到四个月，之后才会面临更严重的经济困境。

speaker: Host
text_en: So when it. We look at this current situation. I know we've talked in the past about how the global economy is already starting to scream. The idea of this going on for four more months puts us in an even more dire situation. How do you view this current standoff and what it could mean for the economy if the U.S. continues to focus on this blockade?
text: 所以，说到这里，我们再看当前的局势。我知道我们之前聊过，全球经济其实已经开始吃不消了。如果这种情况再持续四个月，局面只会更加严峻。你怎么看现在这种对峙？如果美国继续把重点放在这种封锁上，这对经济可能意味着什么？

speaker: Guest
text_en: A couple points. One is just to go to the Washington Post story about the CIA report. a report. Basically, that report says that the Iranian economy will be wrecked in three to four months, or it'll take three to four months to really wreck the Iranian economy. But that very importantly does not mean that Iran will surrender.
text: 我想说几点。第一，先说 Washington Post 那篇关于 CIA 报告的报道。基本上，那份报告的意思是，Iran 的经济会在三到四个月内被重创，或者说，要三到四个月才能真正把 Iran 经济打垮。但非常重要的一点是，这并不意味着 Iran 会投降。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And I believe that Iran will not surrender, that you can inflict much more punishment beyond those three or four months. And the Iranians will not surrender. I think the historical record is quite clear on this issue,
text: 而且我认为 Iran 不会投降。就算在那三四个月之后继续施加更大的打击，Iran 人也不会投降。我觉得历史记录在这个问题上已经说得很清楚了，

speaker: Guest
text_en: that societies in wartime can absorb enormous amounts of punishment, number one. And number two, the Iranians are facing an existential threat. They're up against two genocidal states, the United States and Israel,
text: 第一，战争中的社会可以承受极其巨大的打击。第二，Iran 面对的是生存性威胁，他们对上的，是两个带有种族灭绝性质的国家——美国和 Israel。

speaker: Guest
text_en: and they are committed to destroying Iran, to wrecking it, to making Iran look like Syria. And given... Iran's emphasis on survival, they're not going to surrender. So this is going to go on beyond three to four months. Second point I would make is that the proponents of the blockade are fully aware that it will take a long time to work.
text: 而且他们一心想摧毁 Iran，想把它搞垮，想让 Iran 变得像 Syria 一样。考虑到……Iran 对生存的高度重视，他们是不可能投降的。所以这件事会持续超过三到四个月。第二点我要说的是，主张封锁的人完全清楚，这种办法要很久才会见效。

speaker: Guest
text_en: From their perspective, in other words, they don't think that Iran is going to throw up its hands tomorrow. They understand that it's going to take, even in their story, a long time. And that has led them to put an emphasis on restarting the bombing campaign against Iran. And in other words, the belief is that the blockade in conjunction with a bombing campaign a serious.
text: 换句话说，从他们的角度看，他们并不认为 Iran 明天就会举手投降。他们明白，就连按照他们自己的说法，这也需要很长时间。这也让他们开始强调要重启对 Iran 的轰炸行动。也就是说，他们相信封锁再加上一场轰炸行动，一场严肃的——

speaker: Guest
text_en: bombing campaign will be a formidable one-two punch that will finish off the Iranians and get them to surrender. So I think the reason that you and I both wake up every day wondering whether or not we're going to go up the escalation ladder today or not is because there is a lot of pressure on the administration from the proponents of this war to go up the escalation ladder and to start
text: 轰炸行动，会形成一记强有力的组合拳，彻底击垮 Iran 人，逼他们投降。所以我觉得，为什么你我每天醒来都会想，今天会不会再往升级的阶梯上走一步，就是因为主张这场战争的人一直在向政府施压，要求继续升级，开始

speaker: Guest
text_en: start bombing Iran again because they all understand deep down that the blockade alone is not going to do the trick certainly in a short period of time and as you said the more time goes on the more the economy
text: 重新轰炸 Iran，因为他们内心都明白，光靠封锁是解决不了问题的，至少短期内肯定不行。而且正如你说的，时间拖得越久，经济就越会

speaker: Guest
text_en: begins to move in and crush President Trump and put him in a position where he has no choice but to figure out how to get out of this conflict. So the crushing effects of the continuing war do create an incentive for him to try to escalate.
text: 开始反过来压垮 President Trump，把他逼到一个别无选择的地步，只能想办法怎么退出这场冲突。所以，持续战争带来的沉重后果，确实会给他制造一种升级的动机。

speaker: Guest
text_en: But the problem that he faces is that escalation is not a war-winning strategy. The fact is that we bombed once before and it didn't. And it didn't work. Now, the other side will argue,
text: 但他面临的问题是，升级并不是一种能打赢战争的策略。事实是，我们之前已经轰炸过一次了，结果并没有——并没有奏效。现在，另一方会说，

speaker: Guest
text_en: yes, but the last time we bombed, we did not have simultaneously a blockade on Iran. Now we have the one-two punch. So let's give it a try. And that logic may be enough to push Trump over the line and get him to go up the escalation ladder.
text: 没错，但上次我们轰炸的时候，并没有同时对 Iran 实施封锁。现在我们有这套组合拳了，那就试试看吧。这套逻辑很可能足以把 Trump 推过那条线，让他继续沿着升级阶梯往上走。

speaker: Guest
text_en: But as I have long argued, that's not going to work. The fact is, as I said before, you can inflict. flicked massive punishment on the Iranians and given their strategic situation, they're not going to quit.
text: 但正如我一直以来所说的，那是行不通的。事实是，就像我之前说的，你可以对 Iran 人施加巨大的惩罚，但考虑到他们的战略处境，他们是不会罢手的。

speaker: Host
text_en: Yeah, exactly. As you said, as I'm watching the Trump administration, it's almost more concerning to me, the people that are in his ear that are telling him, oh, yeah, yeah, we can do it now. We've got this blockcade. We've got all these different military options. But exactly as you're describing it there,
text: 对，完全没错。就像你说的，我在看 Trump 政府的时候，几乎更让我担心的是他身边那些给他出主意的人，他们跟他说，哦，对对对，我们现在能干了。我们有这个封锁。我们还有各种不同的军事选项。但就像你刚才描述的那样，

speaker: Host
text_en: look, Iran is holding on to control of the Strait of Hormuz with everything they have. That is not going to change. I mean, the IRGC announced this week that they will be expanding their control of the Strait, going right up to the UAE's port,
text: 你看，Iran 正在拼尽全力维持对 Hormuz 海峡的控制，这一点不会改变。我的意思是，IRGC 这周宣布，他们会扩大对海峡的控制，一直推进到 UAE 的港口附近，

speaker: Host
text_en: which you were explaining earlier they're using to bypass this initial blockade by Iran. So when it comes to control of the Strait of Hormuz and the impact that Iran is able to have on the global economy, on all of these different elements. that we all rely on. We obviously have not started feeling anywhere near the pain we are going to feel just yet.
text: 而你刚才也解释过，他们现在正是利用那个港口来绕过 Iran 最初的这轮封锁。所以说到对 Hormuz 海峡的控制，以及 Iran 能够对全球经济、对我们所有人赖以生存的这些不同环节造成的影响，很显然，我们现在感受到的痛苦，离接下来真正要承受的程度还差得远。

speaker: Host
text_en: How important is this vital waterway and do you expect to see the conflict continue to focus on the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. and Iran continue to face off?
text: 这条关键水道到底有多重要？你是否预计，随着 U.S. 和 Iran 继续对峙，冲突的焦点还会继续集中在 Hormuz 海峡上？

speaker: Guest
text_en: You want to remember that to get a deal here so that we can end up putting this. conflict behind us and avoiding the international economy going off a cliff, there are just all sorts of issues that have to be worked out.
text: 你得记住，要想在这里达成协议，把这场冲突抛在身后，并避免国际经济断崖式下跌，还有各种各样的问题必须先解决。

speaker: Guest
text_en: I mean, one of the reasons I'm very pessimistic that you're going to get some sort of meaningful settlement that will allow the international economy not to go off a cliff is because there are just so many different issues.
text: 我的意思是，我之所以非常悲观，不认为你们能达成某种有实质意义的解决方案、从而让国际经济不至于断崖式下跌，就是因为要处理的问题实在太多了。

speaker: Guest
text_en: that have to be settled before you can end the conflict. Now, a lot of focus on the part of the Trump administration is on the nuclear issue. And the nuclear issue by itself is a very complicated issue.
text: 在冲突结束之前，有太多事情必须先谈妥。现在，Trump 政府很大一部分注意力都放在核问题上。而核问题本身就是一个非常复杂的问题。

speaker: Guest
text_en: There's the whole question of what kind of nuclear enrichment capability the Iranians are going to be allowed to have. There's the whole question of what to do with... Those 11 tons of enriched uranium that the Iranians have.
text: 这里面有个核心问题，就是 Iran 将被允许拥有多大程度的铀浓缩能力。还有一个大问题，就是该怎么处理……Iran 手里的那 11 吨浓缩铀。

speaker: Guest
text_en: There's the question that you were raising of the Strait of Hormuz. What's the future of the Strait of Hormuz? Is the United States going to allow Iran to control the Strait of Hormuz and keep a toll booth in that strait for the foreseeable future? What's the future of American military bases in the region? What's the future of reparations?
text: 还有你刚才提到的霍尔木兹海峡问题。霍尔木兹海峡的未来会怎样？美国会不会允许 Iran 在可预见的未来控制霍尔木兹海峡，并在那条海峡里一直设收费站？美国在该地区军事基地的未来是什么？战争赔偿的未来又会怎样？

speaker: Guest
text_en: what's the future of sanctions what's the future of Iran's relationship with Hezbollah Hamas and the Houthis these are all big issues what's the future of the Iranian missile program and so forth and so on so we not only have to work out the nuclear issue and the question of who controls the Strait all these issues have to be addressed And it's hard for me to imagine the United States and Iran.
text: 制裁的未来是什么？Iran 与 Hezbollah、Hamas 和 Houthis 的关系未来会怎样？这些都是重大问题。Iran 导弹计划的未来又如何，等等等等。所以我们不仅要解决核问题和谁控制海峡的问题，所有这些问题都得处理。而且我很难想象美国和 Iran 会在短期内

speaker: Guest
text_en: reaching a deal on these different issues anytime soon and then throw in the Israelis even if the United States and the Iranians are moving toward a deal the Israelis who want to continue the war and want to destroy Iran very much will go to great lengths to undermine the negotiations between the United States and Iran and then when it comes to negotiations there are two big problems there
text: 就这些不同问题达成协议。再把 Israelis 这个因素加进来：即便美国和 Iran 正在朝着达成协议的方向走，那些想继续战争、而且非常想摧毁 Iran 的 Israelis，也会不遗余力地破坏美伊之间的谈判。然后说到谈判，这里面还有两个大问题。

speaker: Host
text_en: One is the trust issue. Do you think the Iranians trust the United States at this point in time? The last time the two sides were negotiating, we all of a sudden launched a war against Iran in the middle of the negotiation.
text: 一个是信任问题。你觉得 Iran 现在还信任美国吗？上一次双方谈判的时候，我们却突然在谈判进行到一半时对 Iran 发动了战争。

speaker: Host
text_en: Can the Iranians trust the United States? And then moreover. Do you think the Trump administration is really capable of executing a negotiation that leads to a meaningful settlement? This is the gang that can't shoot straight.
text: Iran 能信任美国吗？而且更进一步说，你觉得 Trump 政府真的有能力完成一场谈判，并最终达成有实质意义的解决方案吗？这帮人连最基本的事都干不利索。

speaker: Guest
text_en: They couldn't settle the war in Ukraine, right? They can't end the genocide in Gaza. I mean, this is not a highly competent administration that's filled with top rate. diplomats. Instead,
text: 他们连 Ukraine 战争都解决不了，对吧？他们也无法结束 Gaza 的种族灭绝。我的意思是，这可不是什么能力很强的政府，里面也不是坐满了一流外交官。恰恰相反，

speaker: Guest
text_en: they have people like Jared Kushner and Steve Whitcoff in charge. So when you put all these things together, it's just hard to see how you get any meaningful settlement. And if you don't get a meaningful settlement, the economic consequences of this in the months ahead are just going to increase greatly. Almost everybody agrees that
text: 他们让 Jared Kushner 和 Steve Whitcoff 这样的人来负责。所以把这些因素放在一起看，你很难看出怎么可能达成任何有实质意义的解决方案。而如果达不成有实质意义的解决方案，接下来几个月这件事带来的经济后果只会大幅加剧。几乎所有人都同意，

speaker: Guest
text_en: The longer this war goes on, the worse the economic consequences for countries all around the world. And President Trump seems to understand that. He seems very anxious to figure out some way to get out of this pickle that he's in. But as I said, I don't see how he does that. There's just so many factors that weigh against a meaningful agreement. I hope I'm wrong, but I don't think I am.
text: 这场战争拖得越久，世界各国要承受的经济后果就越严重。而 Trump 总统似乎也明白这一点。他看起来非常急于想办法摆脱自己现在的这个困局。但正如我说的，我看不出他要怎么做到。阻碍达成有实质意义协议的因素实在太多了。我希望我是错的，但我觉得我不是。

speaker: Host
text_en: Oh, I'm with you on that one. And that when you look at this and yeah, in addition to the fact that the U.S. only seems to have two diplomats that can do anything here when it comes to the Trump administration,
text: 哦，这点我完全同意你。而且你看这件事，再加上在 Trump 政府这边，美国看起来也就只有两个外交官还能在这事上真正发挥作用。

speaker: Host
text_en: you also have the fact that they were on the brink of a deal multiple times with Iran and each time they decided to attack in the middle of negotiations. Now, while all of that is happening, I do also want to bring up Trump's planned visit to Beijing next week because he's set to have talks with.
text: 还有一点是，他们和 Iran 已经好几次都快谈成协议了，但每一次他们都选择在谈判进行到一半时发动攻击。与此同时，我也想提一下 Trump 计划下周访问 Beijing，因为他将和……举行会谈。

speaker: Host
text_en: Chinese President Xi Jinping. The stakes really couldn't be higher there as you look at the role that China is kind of playing in all of this and where it stands. And we also had Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Arachi in China for talks this week. And there has been, of course, increasing talk about this turning into an energy war, especially with a focus on the Strait of Hormuz.
text: 中国国家主席 Xi Jinping。那边的利害关系真的已经高到不能再高了，尤其是考虑到 China 在整件事里所扮演的某种角色，以及它所处的位置。而且这周 Iranian 外长 Abbas Arachi 也在 China 进行了会谈。当然，现在越来越多人在谈，这件事可能会演变成一场能源战争，尤其焦点集中在 Hormuz 海峡。

speaker: Host
text_en: I mean, as of last year, it was estimated that around half of the world's energy was coming from China. Around half of China's crude oil imports were coming from the Middle East. So how do you see the position that China is playing here, its ability to talk to the U.S., but also this meeting coming at a time when the U.S. is in a position of openly trying to hurt China here?
text: 我的意思是，截至去年，估计全球大约有一半的能源都和 China 有关。China 进口的原油里，大约有一半来自中东。所以你怎么看 China 现在所处的位置、它和美国对话的能力，以及这次会面偏偏发生在美国公开试图打压 China 的这个时候？

speaker: Guest
text_en: Well, I think that President Trump is clearly in a weak position here. I think one of the reasons that he'd like to settle this one quickly.
text: 嗯，我认为 Trump 总统在这件事上显然处于弱势。我觉得他想尽快把这件事了结，其中一个原因就是这个。

speaker: Guest
text_en: One of the reasons he wants to get the Iranians to buy on to his plan and start seriously negotiating over his plan is so that he can go to Beijing and look like he's in the process of putting an end to this war and he can look like a peacemaker and he can present a positive image to Xi Jinping. But the fact is, that's unlikely to happen.
text: 他之所以想让 Iranian 接受他的方案，并开始围绕他的方案认真谈判，其中一个原因就是，这样他去 Beijing 的时候就能显得自己正在推动结束这场战争，能把自己塑造成一个和平缔造者，也能在 Xi Jinping 面前展现一个更正面的形象。但现实是，这种情况不太可能发生。

speaker: Guest
text_en: The idea that he's going to be in a strong position because the Iranians are caving into him and willing to agree to some sort of settlement on Trump's terms is not going to happen.
text: 说他会处在一个强势位置，因为 Iranian 会向他让步，并愿意按 Trump 的条件接受某种解决方案，这种事是不可能发生的。

speaker: Guest
text_en: So he's going to go to Beijing in a weak position because the war is going to be continuing and Xi Jinping is going to be in a position. position where he can remind President Trump that he is responsible for starting this war,
text: 所以他去 Beijing 的时候会处于弱势，因为战争还会继续，而 Xi Jinping 将处在一个可以提醒 Trump 总统：这场战争是他挑起来的位置上。

speaker: Guest
text_en: that it was a fundamental mistake, and that the entire world is suffering, not just China and not just the United States. And therefore, it's imperative that he, President Trump,
text: 这是一个根本性的错误，而且受苦的是整个世界，不只是中国，也不只是美国。因此，当务之急是让他，也就是 President Trump，

speaker: Guest
text_en: end the war as quickly as possible. So what I think Xi Jinping will do is try to make it clear that Trump's responsible for the war. And he bears the burden of ending the war. Xi Jinping will say, I've been very helpful up to now.
text: 尽快结束这场战争。所以我认为 Xi Jinping 会做的是，试图明确表明这场战争是 Trump 的责任，而结束战争的担子也在他身上。Xi Jinping 会说，到目前为止，我已经帮了很多忙。

speaker: Guest
text_en: I've put pressure on both the Pakistanis and the Iranians to meet in Islamabad last month. But there are limits to what I can do. You're in the driver's seat, President Trump, and you have to end this war.
text: 上个月我已经向巴基斯坦和伊朗双方施压，促成他们在 Islamabad 会面。但我能做的也是有限的。现在方向盘在你手里，President Trump，这场战争得由你来结束。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And of course, President Trump doesn't know how he can end the war. That's the problem he faces. So you can see that. that President Trump will be in a very awkward position when he goes to Beijing. Make one final point.
text: 当然，President Trump 并不知道该怎么结束这场战争。这就是他面临的问题。所以你可以看出来，President Trump 去北京时会处在一个非常尴尬的位置。最后再说一点。

speaker: Guest
text_en: The United States is deeply involved, obviously, in this war in the Middle East. It's also deep. deeply involved in the war in Ukraine. It's trying to wean itself off that war and shift the burden to the Europeans. But nevertheless,
text: 很显然，美国已经深度卷入了中东这场战争。它也深深卷入了乌克兰战争。它正试图逐步从那场战争中抽身，把负担转移给欧洲人。不过，

speaker: Guest
text_en: the United States remains a key player in that war. That means, given that we're involved in Europe and in the Persian Gulf, that the last thing President Trump wants is a crisis in East Asia. That would be a bridge too far, to put it mildly. So I think that means that President Trump will go to great lengths to be nice.
text: 美国仍然是那场战争中的关键参与者。这意味着，既然我们在欧洲和波斯湾都已卷入，President Trump 最不想看到的就是东亚再爆发危机。往轻了说，那都太过头了。所以我认为，这意味着 President Trump 会尽一切努力表现得友好。

speaker: Guest
text_en: to Xi Jinping and to smooth over ruffled feathers and to make it clear that he wants, he President Trump wants to have good relations with China. Because again, the last thing that President Trump needs is a crisis or a conflict, a shooting conflict in East Asia. So again, you see that Xi Jinping is in the driver's seat here.
text: 对 Xi Jinping 友好，安抚被冒犯的情绪，并明确表示他，也就是 President Trump，想和中国保持良好关系。因为说到底，President Trump 最不需要的，就是东亚出现危机或冲突，尤其是爆发热战。所以你又能看出来，在这件事上，Xi Jinping 是掌握主动的一方。

speaker: Host
text_en: Yeah, and I think that that's an incredibly important point as we watch this meeting come together. And I wanted to get into kind of the position of U.S. foreign policy strategy here, because exactly as you're describing it there,
text: 对，而且我觉得，随着这次会面逐渐成形，这是一个极其重要的点。我也想进一步谈谈美国外交政策战略在这里所处的位置，因为就像你刚才描述的那样，

speaker: Host
text_en: the U.S. is trying to put this focus over here on West Asia, on the war in Iran, and in doing so they're having to take away from their focus on Europe, from their focus on East Asia.
text: 美国正试图把重心转到西亚、转到对伊朗的战争上，而这么做就意味着它不得不削弱对欧洲的关注，也削弱对东亚的关注。

speaker: Host
text_en: And it reminds me of the fact that the U.S. gleefully encouraged its allies in Europe. up to cut off their reliance on cheap natural resources from Russia and to instead increase their reliance on the U.S. and also the Middle East,
text: 这让我想到，美国当初兴高采烈地鼓动它在欧洲的盟友切断对俄罗斯廉价自然资源的依赖，转而增加对美国以及中东的依赖。

speaker: Host
text_en: now the U.S. is pursuing this war against Iran that not only hurts Europe, but it hurts U.S. allies in the Persian Gulf and U.S. allies in East Asia. And so when we look at the future of the U.S. and its ability to project power,
text: 而现在，美国又在推进这场针对伊朗的战争，这不仅伤害了欧洲，也伤害了美国在波斯湾的盟友和美国在东亚的盟友。所以当我们看美国的未来，以及它投射力量的能力时，

speaker: Host
text_en: it feels as though right now the U.S. is making all of its all of its top allies even weaker while at the same time also making itself weaker. How do you view this overall strategy and kind of what the U.S. is capable of here?
text: 感觉就像美国现在一边把自己所有最重要的盟友都搞得更虚弱，一边也在把自己搞得更虚弱。你怎么看这种整体战略，以及美国在这里到底还有多大能力？

speaker: Guest
text_en: I'm going to just basically repeat what you said because I agree completely and I'll just try to put a finer point on it or reinforce what you said. But, you know, as you described it, there are three sets of allies here that are at play, our allies in East Asia. our allies in Europe and our allies in the Gulf. And as a result of what happened on February 28th when we went to war against Iran,
text: 我基本上就是重复你刚才说的话，因为我完全同意，我只是想把这个观点说得更明确一点，或者说进一步强化你的判断。不过，按你的描述，这里牵涉到三组盟友：我们在东亚的盟友、我们在欧洲的盟友，以及我们在海湾地区的盟友。而自从2月28日我们对伊朗开战以来，

speaker: Guest
text_en: we have badly hurt all of our allies just to go to East Asia because we are running out of weapons and ammunition in the Persian Gulf. We are actually moving patriots.
text: 我们已经严重伤害了所有这些盟友。先说东亚，因为我们在波斯湾的武器和弹药正在见底。我们实际上正在调动 Patriot。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And add missile batteries out of East Asia to the Gulf, we are moving or we have moved a marine expeditionary unit out of Japan to the Gulf. We're in effect pivoting away from East Asia and putting our allies in a precarious position.
text: 还把导弹炮台从东亚调往海湾，我们正在把、或者说已经把一支海军陆战队远征部队从日本调往海湾。实际上，我们是在从东亚抽身，这让我们的盟友处在一个很危险的境地。

speaker: Guest
text_en: So we're doing real damage there. And furthermore, and this cannot be underestimated. The economic effects of this war have been felt much more in East Asia than either Europe or in the United States.
text: 所以我们在那里造成了实实在在的伤害。而且，这一点怎么强调都不为过：这场战争带来的经济影响，在东亚的感受要比在欧洲甚至在美国本土都更强烈。

speaker: Guest
text_en: So if you look at Japan and South Korea, they're really hurting as a result of the economic consequences of the war. So just to sum up the situation in East Asia, we're damaging our allies both from a strategic perspective.
text: 所以如果你看看 Japan 和 South Korea，就会发现它们确实因为这场战争带来的经济后果而深受打击。简单总结一下 East Asia 的局势：从战略角度看，我们正在伤害自己的盟友。

speaker: Guest
text_en: From a strategic point of view, because we're pivoting away from East Asia and from an economic point of view because of all the pain that we're causing them on the economic front, then you go to Europe, you describe the situation well there,
text: 从战略层面来说，是因为我们正在把重心从 East Asia 转移开；从经济层面来说，则是因为我们在经济战线上给他们造成了大量痛苦。然后再看 Europe，那里的情况你已经描述得很清楚了。

speaker: Guest
text_en: the Ukraine war. was a disaster for the European economies. And this war now is doing significant damage, especially in Britain, and will do greater damage with the passage of time. Then you go to the Gulf. On February 27th, we had this very...
text: Ukraine 战争对欧洲各国经济来说是一场灾难。而现在这场战争正在造成重大伤害，尤其是在 Britain，而且随着时间推移，伤害还会进一步加深。然后再看海湾地区。2 月 27 日，我们曾有一个看起来非常——

speaker: Guest
text_en: What looked like impressive alliance structure involving the GCC states, the Gulf Council states, Cooperation Council states, and we had bases in almost all those states.
text: 看起来很强大的联盟架构，涉及 GCC 国家，也就是 Gulf Cooperation Council 各国，而且我们在这些国家中的几乎所有国家都设有基地。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And it looked like we really had a formidable deterrent structure in place since the war began. Almost all those bases have either been badly damaged or destroyed. These are the American bases. And furthermore, instead of providing security for. Our allies in the Gulf, our bases have acted like a giant magnet that attracts Iranian ballistic missiles, cruise missiles, and drones.
text: 而且看起来，自战争开始以来，我们确实建立起了一套相当强大的威慑体系。可这些基地里，几乎所有基地不是严重受损，就是已经被摧毁了。这些都是美国基地。更进一步说，我们在海湾的基地非但没有给盟友提供安全，反而像一块巨大的磁铁，把 Iranian 的弹道导弹、巡航导弹和无人机都吸引了过来。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Furthermore, you see all sorts of fractures taking place among the GCC states. And the most obvious example is what's happened with the UAE and Saudi Arabia. It seems that the UAE views Saudi Arabia as very much. every much as big a threat as Iran.
text: 此外，你还能看到 GCC 国家之间出现了各种裂痕。最明显的例子，就是 UAE 和 Saudi Arabia 之间发生的事。看起来，UAE 认为 Saudi Arabia 对自己的威胁，几乎和 Iran 一样大。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And the Saudis probably view the UAE as an even bigger threat than Iran. And that's just the beginning. So our alliance structure, our basing structure in the Gulf has been badly damaged,
text: 而 Saudi 方面可能觉得，UAE 对自己的威胁甚至比 Iran 还要大。而这还只是个开始。所以我们的联盟体系、我们在海湾的基地体系，已经遭到了严重破坏。

speaker: Guest
text_en: to put it mildly. So to add all these things up, if you look at what happened to our alliance structure in East Asia, in Europe, and in the Persian Gulf. We've done serious damage to all three of these alliance structures. At the same time, we are pursuing a losing war.
text: 这还是往轻了说。所以把这些都加在一起看，如果你看看我们在 East Asia、Europe 和 Persian Gulf 的联盟体系发生了什么，就会发现我们已经对这三套联盟体系都造成了严重伤害。与此同时，我们还在打一场注定会输的战争。

speaker: Host
text_en: Yeah, that's the scary part there, right? Especially when you look at the position that the U.S. is in, all of the damage it has caused. And that brings me to my next point, which is kind of what comes next when you look at the overall multipolar world. You've got China and Russia in this position where... where they're being very careful right now, right?
text: 对，这才是最可怕的部分，对吧？尤其是当你看看美国现在所处的位置，以及它已经造成的所有破坏。这也引出了我的下一个问题，就是当你放眼整个多极世界时，接下来会发生什么。中国和俄罗斯现在处在这样一个位置上——他们目前都非常谨慎，对吧？

speaker: Host
text_en: The fact that you have Trump's trip to China still happening, you've got Beijing in this position where they're kind of carefully handling someone who we know is a bit volatile, and that raises concerns about what the U.S. could do,
text: 你看，Trump 访问中国的行程还在推进，北京现在有点像是在小心应对一个我们都知道情绪不太稳定的人，而这也让人担心美国接下来可能会做什么，

speaker: Host
text_en: what Israel could do if this escalates to the point where they realize that not only are they not in the driver's seat, but there's no way for them to get back to it, and you have this growing multipolar world that is continuing to put them in their place.
text: 以及如果局势升级到某个程度，Israel 会做什么——到那时他们会意识到，自己不仅已经不在主导位置上了，而且也根本没有办法再回到那个位置，而这个不断壮大的多极世界还会继续让他们认清自己的位置。

speaker: Host
text_en: So the last thing I wanted to bring up, as you watch this trip kind of come together in the next week, and as we watch the possibility of the U.S., whether it's returning to strikes or an attempted special ops ground invasion of sorts in Iran, what are you going to be keeping an eye on when it comes to that kind of overall strategy of the multipolar world in dealing? dealing with the U.S.
text: 所以我最后想提的是，接下来这一周，随着这次访问逐渐成形，以及我们继续观察美国是否会重新发动打击，或者试图在 Iran 发起某种特种作战式的地面入侵，你会重点关注哪些信号？尤其是从多极世界整体应对美国的战略这个角度来看。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Yeah, that's a great question. If you listen to me talk up to now, especially when you and I were both describing the situation with our allies in the different regions of the world,
text: 对，这是个很好的问题。如果你听我刚才讲到现在，尤其是你我都在描述美国盟友在世界不同地区的处境时，

speaker: Guest
text_en: one might get the impression that these are three distinct regions and there are few interconnections among them. But if you listen carefully to what I was saying and you really think about it. These regions are interconnected in all sorts of ways.
text: 可能会让人觉得这是三个彼此分开的地区，它们之间联系不多。但如果你仔细听我刚才说的内容，再认真想一想，就会发现这些地区在很多方面都是相互联结的。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And when you talk about the war in the Gulf, for example, it's very important to understand that the Russians and the Chinese are both helping Iran wage the war. I believe one of the reasons that the missile campaign, excuse me, that Iran has launched against the Gulf states and against Israel and against America. American military bases.
text: 比如说，当你谈到海湾地区的战争时，非常重要的一点是要明白，俄罗斯和中国都在帮助 Iran 打这场仗。我认为，Iran 针对海湾国家、Israel 以及美国——美国军事基地——发起的导弹行动之所以如此有效，其中一个原因就在于此。

speaker: Guest
text_en: One of the reasons it has been so effective is because of the intelligence that the Russians have been providing the Iranians. And of course, the Chinese have been providing weaponry and military help as well. Furthermore, as you well know,
text: 它之所以这么有效，其中一个原因就是俄罗斯一直在向 Iran 提供情报。当然，中国也一直在提供武器和其他军事支持。此外，正如你很清楚的那样，

speaker: Guest
text_en: And as you pointed out, China is heavily dependent on imports, imported oil and other kinds of imports as well from the Persian Gulf. So you see that China and Russia are inextricably bound up in the events that are taking place in the Gulf.
text: 而且正如你指出的那样，中国在很大程度上依赖进口，包括从波斯湾进口石油以及其他各类物资。所以你会看到，中国和俄罗斯都与海湾正在发生的事件紧密相连，根本分不开。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And, of course, the Russians and the Chinese work together all the time, especially in. in Ukraine, where the Chinese have helped the Russians in a variety of ways. So all of these conflict situations are interconnected. And the great danger is that through some happenstance, we'll end up in a war.
text: 当然，俄罗斯人和中国人一直都在合作，尤其是在乌克兰问题上，中国以各种方式帮助了俄罗斯。所以所有这些冲突局势彼此都是相互关联的。最大的危险在于，某个偶然事件可能会把我们拖入战争。

speaker: Guest
text_en: In the Persian Gulf that involves Russia and China or Russia or China, in addition to the Persian Gulf states, in addition to Iran. So my point is we have to be remarkably. careful here to make sure that the conflict in the Persian Gulf doesn't escalate to the point where both the Chinese and the Russians come in. And I think that's a real danger.
text: 在波斯湾，这场战争可能会牵涉俄罗斯和中国，或者俄罗斯、或者中国，除此之外还会牵涉波斯湾国家以及 Iran。所以我的意思是，我们在这里必须格外谨慎，确保波斯湾的冲突不会升级到把中国和俄罗斯都卷进来的地步。我认为这是真实存在的危险。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And given the fact that it doesn't appear that you're going to be able to settle this conflict permanently. and that it's going to linger for a long, long time and that China and Russia are not going to lose their interest in the Persian Gulf. The danger of escalation to the point where all the great powers or two of the great powers get involved is very real.
text: 而且考虑到这场冲突看起来不太可能被永久解决，它会长期拖延下去，而中国和俄罗斯也不会失去对波斯湾的兴趣。那么，局势升级到所有大国、或者其中两个大国卷入其中的危险，是非常现实的。

speaker: Guest
text_en: So the point I'm making here in general is we want to be aware that there are three different areas of the world and you can look at them in isolation, but ultimately they're interrelated or interconnected in very important ways.
text: 所以我总体上想表达的是，我们要意识到，世界上有三个不同的地区，你可以把它们分开来看，但归根结底，它们之间以非常重要的方式相互关联、彼此联动。

speaker: Host
text_en: Oh, absolutely. And exactly as you're describing it there, it is raising a lot of concerns about all of the different ways that this conflict could escalate. And the U.S. is involved in every single element here,
text: 哦，绝对如此。正如你刚才描述的那样，这也引发了很多担忧，因为这场冲突有各种不同的升级路径。而美国在这里每一个环节里都有介入，

speaker: Host
text_en: picking all of these fights and now starting to see a lot of the consequences of its foreign policy actions here. I know that there is certainly a lot at stake here all around. And I really appreciate you taking the time to join me.
text: 到处挑起这些争端，现在也开始看到其外交政策行为带来的许多后果。我知道这里各方的利害关系都非常重大。也非常感谢你抽时间来参加我的节目。

speaker: Host
text_en: me today to break down all of the latest here. John Mearsheimer, an author, international relations expert, and professor at the University of Chicago. Thank you so much for your time and insight.
text: 今天来帮我梳理这里最新的局势。John Mearsheimer，作家、国际关系专家、University of Chicago 教授。非常感谢你抽出时间，也感谢你的见解。

speaker: Guest
text_en: My pleasure, Rachel. Thank you for having me on the show.
text: 不客气，Rachel。谢谢你邀请我来上节目。

speaker: Host
text_en: If anything in this video resonated with you, be sure to like it, share it with your friends, leave a comment. And as always, don't forget to subscribe.
text: 如果这期视频里有任何内容让你产生共鸣，记得点赞、分享给朋友，再留个评论。还有老规矩，别忘了订阅。

speaker: Host
text_en: And if you want to keep up with all of my work, make sure that you're subscribed to my page on Substack. That's rachelblubbins.substack.com. That's where you'll find ad-free videos and new weekly episodes of my exclusive series for paid subscribers.
text: 如果你想持续关注我的所有内容，记得订阅我在 Substack 上的页面，网址是 rachelblubbins.substack.com。你可以在那里看到无广告视频，以及我为付费订阅用户推出的独家系列每周新一期节目。

speaker: Host
text_en: called Sanctioned. You can also catch Sanctioned over on my page on Patreon. That's patreon.com slash Rachel Blevins. As always, thank y'all so much for all of your support and I'll see you next time.
text: 这个系列叫 Sanctioned。你也可以在我 Patreon 的页面上收看 Sanctioned，网址是 patreon.com slash Rachel Blevins。还是老样子，非常感谢大家一直以来的支持，我们下次见。
